# BigCommerce current — Tech Pack

## 1. Fingerprinting Signals

| Signal | Type | Value | Confidence |
|--------|------|-------|------------|
| `cdn11.bigcommerce.com` in HTML | HTML source | CDN hostname in `<script src>` or `<link href>` | Definitive |
| `cdn5.bigcommerce.com` in HTML | HTML source | Alternate CDN hostname in `<script src>` or `<link href>` | Definitive |
| `window.BCData` JS object | JS global | Object present in page source on every storefront page | Definitive |
| `window.BCData.cart_id` | JS global | Cart UUID string present when active cart exists | Definitive |
| `bigcommerce.com` in page source or headers | HTML source / headers | Platform URL embedded in HTML or `X-Powered-By` header | High |
| `X-Powered-By: BigCommerce` response header | HTTP header | Present on some older store configurations | High |
| `bc_` prefixed cookies | Cookie | Session and tracking cookies set by the storefront | High |
| `window.__webpack_require__` with BC chunk patterns | JS global | Webpack runtime with BigCommerce-specific chunk naming | High |
| Cornerstone theme assets: `/assets/js/theme/` | HTML source | Script paths referencing the Cornerstone theme directory | High |
| `data-tag` attributes on HTML elements | HTML source | Stencil theme HTML data attributes for event tracking | Medium |

**Extract store hash and BCData object from HTML:**

```bash
# Extract store hash from CDN URL pattern — do this first, it's required for Management API:
curl -s https://STORE_DOMAIN/ | grep -oP 'cdn11\.bigcommerce\.com/s-\K[a-z0-9]+'

# Extract the full BCData inline block from page source:
curl -s https://STORE_DOMAIN/ | grep -oP 'window\.BCData\s*=\s*\{[^<]+' | head -c 500

# Extract store_id from BCData:
curl -s https://STORE_DOMAIN/ | grep -oP '"store_id":\K[^,}]+'

# Check X-Powered-By and other identifying headers:
curl -I https://STORE_DOMAIN/
```

## 2. Default API Surfaces

| Endpoint | Method | Auth | Notes |
|----------|--------|------|-------|
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/graphql` | POST | `Authorization: Bearer {gql_token}` | Storefront GraphQL API; token from page source or Management API |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/carts` | GET | None (session cookie) | Active cart; session-bound, no explicit auth required |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/carts/{id}/redirects` | GET | None (session cookie) | Cart checkout redirect URL |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/products` | GET | None | Storefront products; public |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/categories` | GET | None | Category tree; public |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/subscriptions` | GET | None (session cookie) | Subscription data for logged-in customer |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/customer/current.jwt?app_client_id={clientId}` | GET | None (session cookie) | JWT for currently logged-in customer; 404 if guest |
| `https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{store_hash}/v3/catalog/products` | GET | `X-Auth-Token` | Full product catalog; Management API |
| `https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{store_hash}/v3/catalog/categories` | GET | `X-Auth-Token` | Category tree; Management API |
| `https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{store_hash}/v3/catalog/products/{id}/variants` | GET | `X-Auth-Token` | Product variants; Management API |
| `https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{store_hash}/v2/orders` | GET | `X-Auth-Token` | Orders; v2 endpoint; Management API |
| `https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{store_hash}/v3/customers` | GET | `X-Auth-Token` | Customer records; Management API |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/sitemap.xml` | GET | None | Full site structure; public |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/login.php` | GET/POST | None / Credentials | Customer login; cookie-based sessions |

## 3. Config / Constants Locations

| Location | How to access | Contains |
|----------|---------------|----------|
| `window.BCData` | View page source or browser console | Cart ID, customer data, store ID, GraphQL token, currency |
| `window.BCData.gql_token` | View page source or browser console | Autogenerated Storefront GraphQL Bearer token; scoped to current session |
| `window.BCData.cart_id` | View page source or browser console | Active cart UUID; use with `/api/storefront/carts/{id}` |
| `window.BCData.customer` | Browser console | Logged-in customer object (id, email, group) if authenticated |
| `window.__webpack_require__` | Browser console | Webpack runtime; chunk IDs expose BigCommerce build artifacts |
| Inline `<script>window.BCData` block | View page source | Full BCData JSON available without executing JS |
| CDN URL `cdn11.bigcommerce.com/s-{hash}/` | View page source | Store hash embedded in every CDN asset URL |
| Stencil `data-*` attributes in HTML | View page source | `data-product-id`, `data-cart-item-add`, `data-tag` expose IDs without API calls |
| `/assets/js/theme/global.js` | View page source / GET request | Cornerstone theme JS; confirms Stencil theme type |

## 4. Auth Patterns

| Pattern | Location | Notes |
|---------|----------|-------|
| `Authorization: Bearer {gql_token}` | HTTP request header | Storefront GraphQL API JWT; obtain from `window.BCData.gql_token` in page source |
| `X-Auth-Token: {api_key}` | HTTP request header | Management API v3 (preferred); API key from store Admin → API Accounts |
| `X-Auth-Token: {api_key}` + `X-Auth-Client: {client_id}` | HTTP request headers | Management API v2 (legacy); client_id required for v2 endpoints |
| Session cookie (BC session) | Cookie jar | Storefront cart and customer session; required for `/api/storefront/carts` |
| `bc_*` cookies | Cookie jar | BigCommerce session tracking cookies; set on first visit |
| Customer JWT via `/customer/current.jwt` | GET response body | Signed JWT for identifying the logged-in customer; requires `app_client_id` query param and API account client_secret for decoding |

**Storefront GraphQL token acquisition — extract from page source:**

```bash
# Method 1: grep for the gql_token field in the BCData block:
curl -s https://STORE_DOMAIN/ | grep -oP '"gql_token":"\K[^"]+'

# Method 2: extract the full inline BCData object and inspect it:
curl -s https://STORE_DOMAIN/ | grep -oP 'window\.BCData\s*=\s*\{[^<]+' | head -c 1000

# Once token is obtained, use it as a Bearer token:
curl -s -X POST https://STORE_DOMAIN/graphql \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {gql_token}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"query":"{ site { products { edges { node { name sku } } } } }"}'
```

Note: The Storefront GraphQL token is NOT a secret. BigCommerce embeds it in the page HTML for client-side use. Finding it in the page source is expected behavior, not a misconfiguration.

**Management API token acquisition:** Create an API Account in the BigCommerce Admin (Settings → API Accounts). The `X-Auth-Token` value is the Access Token shown at creation time.

## 5. JS Bundle Patterns

| Path | Content |
|------|---------|
| `https://cdn11.bigcommerce.com/s-{hash}/js/theme-bundle.{hash}.js` | Compiled Stencil/Cornerstone theme bundle; CDN-hosted |
| `https://cdn11.bigcommerce.com/s-{hash}/assets/js/theme/global.js` | Cornerstone global JS; fingerprint signal for Stencil theme |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/assets/js/theme/global.js` | Alternate path for Cornerstone global JS on custom domain |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/assets/js/theme/{page-type}.js` | Page-specific Stencil JS; e.g., `product.js`, `cart.js`, `category.js` |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/assets/js/app.js` | Main application bundle |
| `https://STORE_DOMAIN/cdn/compiled/assets/*.js` | Compiled production assets; alternative CDN path |
| `https://cdn5.bigcommerce.com/r-{hash}/bigcommerce.*.js` | BigCommerce platform JS loaded from cdn5 CDN |

Extract actual asset URLs from HTML `<script src>` tags — the store hash and build hash in CDN paths are store-specific and must be read from the live page. Stencil/Cornerstone stores host all JS on the BigCommerce CDN; no local webpack dev server applies to production.

## 6. Source Map Patterns

CDN-hosted Stencil theme JS typically does **not** include source maps in production. BigCommerce's CDN serves minified bundles without accompanying `.map` files in standard configurations.

**Check for source maps on CDN assets:**

```bash
# Probe for .map file next to a known CDN script:
# First extract a real script URL from page source:
SCRIPT_URL=$(curl -s https://STORE_DOMAIN/ | grep -oP 'https://cdn11\.bigcommerce\.com/[^"]+\.js' | head -1)
# Then probe the .map file:
curl -I "${SCRIPT_URL}.map"

# Check for SourceMap or X-SourceMap response headers on JS assets:
curl -I "${SCRIPT_URL}" | grep -i 'sourcemap\|source-map'
```

**Headless BigCommerce (Next.js / React front end):**

```bash
# Headless storefronts built with Next.js may expose source maps if not stripped at build:
curl -I https://STORE_DOMAIN/_next/static/chunks/pages/index.js.map

# Check for Vite-based builds (common with BC headless starters):
curl -I https://STORE_DOMAIN/assets/index.js.map
```

Source maps are almost always absent on standard Stencil/Cornerstone stores. Headless deployments may expose them depending on build configuration.

## 7. Common Plugins & Extensions

| App/Integration | API it adds | Detection signal |
|-----------------|-------------|------------------|
| Klaviyo | Email/SMS marketing events | `//a.klaviyo.com/` script in HTML; `_kx` cookie |
| Yotpo | Product reviews widget | `staticw2.yotpo.com` script tag |
| ShipStation | Shipping and fulfillment integration | `ss-{store}` references in order management; no storefront JS signal |
| Akamai CDN | WAF and CDN layer in front of BC | `akamaiedge.net` in DNS resolution or `X-Check-Cacheable` header |
| Attentive | SMS popup opt-in | `cdn.attn.tv` script tag |
| Gorgias | Live chat widget | `config.gorgias.com` script tag |
| LoyaltyLion | Loyalty points widget | `cdn.loyaltylion.com` script tag |
| Recharge | Subscription payments | `rechargepayments.com` in scripts |
| Okendo | Product reviews | `cdn.okendo.io` script tag |
| BigCommerce B2B Edition | B2B buyer portal, additional REST endpoints under `/b2b/` | `window.B2BQuote` or `/b2b/` route patterns |

## 8. Known Public Data

| Endpoint | Data | Notes |
|----------|------|-------|
| `POST /graphql` with products query | Product catalog with name, SKU, prices, images | Requires Storefront GraphQL Bearer token from page source |
| `POST /graphql` with categoryTree query | Full category hierarchy with paths | Requires Storefront GraphQL Bearer token |
| `GET /api/storefront/products` | Storefront product list | Public; no auth required |
| `GET /api/storefront/categories` | Storefront category list | Public; no auth required |
| `GET /api/storefront/carts` | Current session cart with items and totals | Session-bound; no explicit auth, but requires active session cookie |
| `GET /sitemap.xml` | All product, category, and page URLs | Best starting point for enumerating store content |
| `GET /customer/current.jwt?app_client_id={id}` | Signed JWT for logged-in customer (id, email) | Returns 404 for guest shoppers; requires valid `app_client_id` |

**Key Storefront GraphQL queries:**

```graphql
# Product listing (requires Bearer token):
{ site { products { edges { node { name sku prices { price { value currencyCode } } } } } } }

# Category tree:
{ site { categoryTree { name path children { name path } } } }

# Authenticated customer info (requires customer impersonation token):
{ customer { firstName lastName email } }
```

## 9. Probe Checklist

- [ ] `HEAD https://STORE_DOMAIN/` — check response headers for `X-Powered-By: BigCommerce`, `bigcommerce.com` references, and CDN routing signals
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/` — grep HTML source for `cdn11.bigcommerce.com`, `window.BCData`, and Stencil `data-*` attributes to confirm BigCommerce fingerprint
- [ ] Extract store hash from CDN URL pattern — `curl -s {site} | grep -oP 'cdn11\.bigcommerce\.com/s-\K[a-z0-9]+'` — store hash is required for all Management API calls; do this early
- [ ] Extract `window.BCData` block from page source — capture `cart_id`, `store_id`, `gql_token`, and `customer` fields
- [ ] Extract `window.BCData.gql_token` — `curl -s {site} | grep -oP '"gql_token":"\K[^"]+'`
- [ ] `POST https://STORE_DOMAIN/graphql` with `Authorization: Bearer {gql_token}` — test Storefront GraphQL with a simple products query
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/carts` — check active cart (session-bound; returns empty array if no cart)
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/products` — public storefront product list; no auth required
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/api/storefront/categories` — public storefront category tree; no auth required
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/sitemap.xml` — enumerate full store URL structure: products, categories, pages
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/customer/current.jwt?app_client_id={clientId}` — attempt customer JWT; expect 404 for guest sessions
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/assets/js/theme/global.js` — confirm Cornerstone/Stencil theme; check for JS globals and theme version
- [ ] Scan HTML `<script src>` tags for third-party signals — Klaviyo, Yotpo, Akamai, Attentive, Gorgias, Okendo
- [ ] Check `Set-Cookie` response headers for `bc_*` cookies — confirm session tracking active
- [ ] Check HTML for Stencil `data-product-id`, `data-cart-item-add`, `data-tag` attributes — extract product IDs without API calls
- [ ] Probe Management API with known store hash: `GET https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{hash}/v3/catalog/products` — expect `401` without `X-Auth-Token`, confirming Management API endpoint is reachable
- [ ] Check for Akamai layer: `curl -I {site} | grep -i 'x-check-cacheable\|akamai\|x-cache'`
- [ ] `GET https://STORE_DOMAIN/login.php` — confirm customer accounts are enabled; check for session login form
- [ ] Probe CDN assets: `curl -I $(curl -s {site} | grep -oP 'https://cdn11\.bigcommerce\.com/[^"]+\.js' | head -1).map` — check for source maps on compiled bundles

## 10. Gotchas

- **Extract the store hash first, from the CDN URL.** The store hash is embedded in every CDN asset URL (`cdn11.bigcommerce.com/s-{hash}/`) and is required for all Management API calls. It is not a secret — it is visible in the HTML of any page. Extract it before attempting any Management API probe.

- **Storefront GraphQL token is NOT a secret.** BigCommerce embeds the GraphQL Bearer token in the page HTML via `window.BCData.gql_token` for client-side use. Finding it in the source is expected behavior by design. However, tokens are scoped to CORS origins and are session-tied; always fetch a fresh token from the live page rather than reusing a cached one.

- **Management API lives at `api.bigcommerce.com`, not on the store domain.** All Management (Admin) REST API calls go to `https://api.bigcommerce.com/stores/{store_hash}/`, not to the storefront domain. Probe it separately. Without an `X-Auth-Token` header, all endpoints return `401`.

- **v3 is preferred; v2 is legacy but active for orders and customers.** The v3 Management API (`/v3/`) is the current standard. However, the Orders endpoint (`/v2/orders`) and some Customer endpoints remain v2-only or richer in v2. Use v3 first; fall back to v2 for order data.

- **Custom domains are the norm — CDN URL is the definitive fingerprint.** Most BigCommerce stores use custom domains (e.g., `store.example.com`) and `*.mybigcommerce.com` will not appear in the HTML. The CDN hostname (`cdn11.bigcommerce.com`) in asset URLs is the most reliable fingerprint — it cannot be hidden by a custom domain.

- **Storefront API tokens are CORS-scoped.** Tokens generated by the Management API (`POST /stores/{hash}/v3/storefront/api-token`) are scoped to declared CORS origins. For probing, always extract the token from the live page source rather than generating one from the Management API, which requires a declared origin.

- **`window.BCData` is on every storefront page — always check it first.** The `window.BCData` inline script block is injected by BigCommerce on every page load and contains cart, customer, store, and token data. Reading it from the HTML source without executing JS is reliable and yields more data than any single API call.

- **Stencil `data-*` attributes expose IDs without API calls.** The Cornerstone/Stencil theme emits `data-product-id`, `data-cart-item-add`, and `data-tag` attributes directly in HTML. Parse these from the rendered page to extract product IDs and event hook targets before touching the API.

- **Customer GraphQL queries require a customer impersonation token, not the standard storefront token.** The `{ customer { ... } }` GraphQL query requires a customer impersonation token (generated server-side via Management API), not the page-embedded `gql_token`. Using the standard token returns null for customer data even when a customer is logged in.

## 12. Framework-Specific Google Dorks

Use these Google search queries to discover exposed endpoints, configuration files, and documentation for this framework.

### Discovery Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `site:{domain} inurl:/api/v3/` | BigCommerce Management API v3 endpoints |
| `site:{domain} inurl:cdn11.bigcommerce.com` | BigCommerce CDN-hosted assets |
| `site:{domain} "bigcommerce" "api"` | BigCommerce API references |
| `site:{domain} "storefront" "token"` | BigCommerce Storefront GraphQL tokens |

### Complete Dork List for BigCommerce

```
# API endpoints
site:{domain} inurl:/graphql
site:{domain} inurl:/api/v3/
site:{domain} inurl:/api/storefront/

# Framework-specific paths
site:{domain} inurl:cdn11.bigcommerce.com
site:{domain} inurl:/login.php

# Configuration files
site:{domain} filetype:js "BCData"
site:{domain} filetype:json "store_hash"

# Documentation/leaks
site:{domain} "BigCommerce" "api" "endpoint"
site:{domain} "gql_token" "Bearer"

# Admin/debug paths
site:{domain} inurl:/admin/
site:{domain} inurl:/customer/current.jwt
```

- **Akamai WAF is common in front of BigCommerce stores.** Many mid-to-large BigCommerce merchants use Akamai for DDoS protection and WAF. Aggressive probing triggers rate limiting and 429 responses. Back off on 429s and space requests. Detect Akamai via `X-Check-Cacheable`, `AkamaiGHost`, or `X-Akamai-*` response headers.

- **`/customer/current.jwt` requires a valid `app_client_id`.** The Current Customer JWT endpoint always requires an `app_client_id` query parameter matching an active API Account's client ID. Without it, the endpoint returns an error. Guest shoppers return 404 regardless of the client ID.

## 11. GitHub Code Search Patterns

Use these queries on GitHub to find custom endpoints, plugin code, and configuration examples for this framework.

### Framework-Specific Queries

| Search Query | What it finds |
|--------------|---------------|
| `"<pattern>" language:<lang> path:<path>` | <description> |

### Example Queries

```bash
# Search for custom endpoints
site:github.com "<framework>" "api" filetype:<ext>

# Search for auth patterns  
site:github.com "<framework>" "auth" "middleware"

# Search for config files
site:github.com "<framework>" "config" "endpoint"
```

## 13. Cross-Cutting OSINT Patterns

These patterns apply across frameworks and should be checked for any detected technology.

### Favicon Hashing

Identify technology stack by hashing favicon and searching Shodan/Censys for same stack:

```bash
# Get favicon hash (mmh3 hash of favicon content)
curl -s "https://{domain}/favicon.ico" | python3 -c "
import sys, hashlib, base64
data = sys.stdin.buffer.read()
# Simple mmh3 hash simulation using Python
import mmh3 2>/dev/null || pip install mmh3
# Or use: python3 -c "import mmh3; print(mmh3.hash(data))"
print('Favicon hash needed for Shodan search: icon_hash')
"

# Search Shodan for same favicon (indicates shadow IT subdomains)
# site:shodan.io search: icon_hash:{hash}
```

**What it reveals:** Hidden subdomains running same framework stack as main site.

### Source Map Discovery

Check for source maps across all JS bundles:

```bash
# Extract all JS bundle URLs from HTML
curl -s "https://{domain}/" | grep -oP 'src="[^"]+\.js[^"]*"' | grep -oP '"[^"]+' | tr -d '"' > js_urls.txt

# Check each for .map file
while read url; do
  map_url="${url}.map"
  status=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "${map_url}")
  [ "$status" = "200" ] && echo "SOURCE MAP: ${map_url}"
done < js_urls.txt
```

**Build tool patterns:**
| Build Tool | Source Map Pattern | Detection |
|------------|-------------------|------------|
| Webpack | `{bundle}.js.map` or `//# sourceMappingURL=` | Check response header `X-SourceMap` |
| Vite | `{name}-[hash].js.map` | Vite manifest `manifest.json` |
| Rollup | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| esbuild | `{bundle}.js.map` | Check `sourceMappingURL` comment |
| Next.js | `/_next/static/chunks/*.js.map` | Only if `productionBrowserSourceMaps: true` |

### Tech Stack → API Pattern Mapping

Auto-map detected frameworks to likely endpoint patterns:

| Framework | Common API Patterns |
|-----------|---------------------|
| Next.js | `/api/*`, `/_next/data/*`, `/api/auth/*`, `/api/trpc/*` |
| WordPress | `/wp-json/*`, `/wp-json/wp/v2/*`, `/wp-admin/admin-ajax.php` |
| Shopify | `/api/2024-10/graphql.json`, `/products.json`, `/collections.json` |
| Rails | `/api/v1/*`, `/assets/*`, `/users/sign_in` |
| Laravel | `/api/*`, `/livewire/message/*`, `/sanctum/csrf-cookie` |
| Strapi | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/api/upload*` |
| Magento | `/rest/V1/*`, `/pub/static/*` |
| Django | `/api/*`, `/admin/*`, `/accounts/*` |

When Phase 3 detects a framework, use this table to prioritize Phase 5/6/7 probes.

### Email Naming Convention Analysis

Extract emails from theHarvester/GitHub results to predict internal subdomains:

```bash
# Sample emails found: john.doe@example.com, jane.smith@example.com
# Predicted subdomains: mail.example.com, smtp.example.com, exchange.example.com

# Common patterns:
# first.last@ → internal.example.com, mail.example.com
# firstinitial+last@ → owa.example.com, outlook.example.com
```

**Add to Phase 9 session brief:** Note email patterns and predicted subdomains.
